title: OpenAI & Anthropic API Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of LLM API integration, streaming, structured output, and cost management.

questions.question: What does setting `temperature=0.0` in a chat completion request achieve?

options.text: It makes responses shorter by filtering out low-probability tokens
It makes responses deterministic — the model always picks the highest probability next token, producing consistent output for caching and testing
It disables the model's safety filters for more direct responses
It limits the response to a maximum of 0 tokens (empty response)

questions.question: Why should you stream LLM responses for chat interfaces instead of waiting for the full response?

options.text: Streaming is cheaper because OpenAI charges less for streamed responses
Streaming shows tokens progressively as they're generated, making the interface feel responsive instead of frozen — users perceive 10 seconds of streaming as faster than 3 seconds of waiting
Non-streaming responses hit a 512 token limit that prevents longer answers
The OpenAI API has a 30-second timeout, so long responses must be streamed

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using `response_format=PydanticModel` (structured output) over asking the LLM to 'return JSON'?

options.text: Structured output is 5x faster than unstructured completion
It guarantees the response is valid JSON conforming to your schema — asking the model to 'return JSON' can still produce malformed or schema-non-compliant responses
It automatically sends schema documentation to improve the model's accuracy
It reduces token costs since JSON is more compact than natural language

questions.question: Why should LLM API calls always be proxied through your backend rather than called directly from a mobile or web frontend?

options.text: The OpenAI API only accepts requests from server-side environments
Exposing the API key in client-side code allows anyone to steal it and make API calls at your expense — the backend acts as an authenticated proxy and rate-limits per-user
Frontend environments cannot handle streaming server-sent events
CORS policies prevent browsers from calling the OpenAI API directly

questions.question: For which use case should you prefer `gpt-4o-mini` over `gpt-4o`?

options.text: Complex multi-step reasoning tasks like PhD-level math or advanced code architecture
High-volume classification, summarization, and extraction tasks where cost efficiency matters and deep reasoning isn't required
Vision tasks that require analyzing images in detail
Tasks requiring real-time streaming since gpt-4o doesn't support streaming
